## [00:00 - 02:17]

Hi, this is Don Crowther with a video about how to get testimonials for your webinar. So one of the most powerful things you can have is a set of testimonials for your webinar. Yes, you should have them for your product, but it's really nice to have them for your webinar itself because then you can use those testimonials to get people to attend your webinar. But how do you go about getting those? Not long ago, I almost stumbled upon a method that I want to tell you about because it really works. So it's language that works something like this. Just before the end of your webinar, say, may I ask you a favor? I always want to improve. So I'd appreciate your feedback. Plus, if you got value out of this webinar, I'd appreciate it if you could give me a testimonial. I could show to other people to get them to attend. And then you continue with something like, so here's what I'd like to do. In a minute, I'm going to end this webinar, but I'm going to let it run for about five more minutes. I won't be talking, but I'll be here reading your comments and testimonials. If you could take a couple of minutes and just give me feedback and or a testimonial about the content of this webinar in the, and then you just either say the chat box or the question box, I'd really appreciate it. Then go ahead and give your inspiring clothes and then stay on the line afterwards. Then, of course, make sure you download your chatter questions at the end so you don't lose them. So here's what will probably happen. You'll get some testimonials. You'll also get some questions that you didn't get during the webinar. And while you're sitting there, go ahead and answer them. Oftentimes, you'll get a cell or two coming from those people. And what I found is that this is usually an amazing five minutes. They're sort of reveling in it. You're feeling great. You've gotten some cells. You got some testimonials come in so you can see those turning into more cells in the future. And it's just a great way to end this entire webinar experience in a mutually beneficial way that just makes you feel great.
